title: silene angustifolia subsp. prostrata
intro: Silene angustifolia subsp. prostrata, also known as creeping silene, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the carnation family and is native to Europe and Western Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of creeping silene include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and protect against infections, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various health issues. Creeping silene can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, and its extracts have been used in traditional medicine for centuries.
